items.title: Declan Rice injury update with England star suffering from neural problem ahead of World Cup last-16 clash vs Mexico
items.summary: DECLAN RICE is battling injury to brave his way through the World Cup. But he will play through the "terrible pain" barrier and is on course to start against Mexico on Sunday night. items.title: Kick-off for England’s World Cup match with Mexico finally CONFIRMED after Fifa sparked chaos with switch plot
items.summary: ENGLAND’S clash against Mexico will STAY as a 1am kick-off after complaints by BOTH teams. Fifa were planning to move the World Cup showdown to SIX HOURS earlier with severe thunderstorms scheduled to washout Mexico City. items.title: Egypt eliminate Australia from World Cup after penalty shoot-out drama
items.summary: Egypt are into the last 16 of the World Cup after beating Australia in a dramatic penalty shoot-out in Dallas.

items.title: Mo Salah breaks down in tears live on BBC after historic Egypt World Cup win moments after scoring Panenka penalty
items.summary: MOHAMED SALAH broke down in tears live on the BBC after guiding Egypt to their first-ever World Cup knockout win. items.title: Postecoglou announced as Al Nassr head coach
items.summary: Ange Postecoglou has signed a two-year contract as coach of Saudi Pro League champions Al Nassr, linking up with Cristiano Ronaldo at the Riyadh-based team.

items.title: Ancelotti: SAF is only manager more qualified
items.summary: Brazil manager Carlo Ancelotti had a reply to social media doubters in Brazil on Friday, saying legendary former Manchester United manager Sir Alex Ferguson is the only manager more qualified than he is.

items.title: England vs Mexico WILL kick-off at 1am Monday
items.summary: England's World Cup last-16 tie with Mexico will remain a 1am kick-off on Monday after confusion over a possible time move.

items.title: England-Mexico kick-off unchanged after Fifa U-turn
items.summary: England's World Cup last-16 tie against Mexico will kick off as scheduled at 01:00 BST on Monday.

items.title: FIFA: Croatia VAR call vs. Portugal was correct
items.summary: FIFA has confirmed that Croatia's equaliser against Portugal was correctly ruled out for offside due to technology housed inside the World Cup ball.

items.title: Nagelsmann out as Germany boss, Klopp lined up
items.summary: Former Liverpool manager Jurgen Klopp said Friday he has had discussions with the German federation about taking over as national team coach after Julian Nagelsmann's exit.

items.title: Agent: Chicago Fire willing to let Mbokazi move
items.summary: Chicago Fire FC centre-back Mbekezeli Mbokazi is understood to be attracting interest from Premier League club Nottingham Forest, but ESPN sources have said that no serious offers have been tabled.

items.title: Egypt beat Roos on PKs for 1st-ever knockout win
items.summary: Egypt beat Australia 4-2 in a penalty shootout Friday to secure the nation's first win in a knockout round match at the FIFA World Cup.

items.title: Sources: Mexico vs. England to KO six hours early
items.summary: Mexico's World Cup round-of-16 clash against England will kickoff at 6 p.m. local time as originally scheduled in Mexico City on Sunday, sources have told ESPN, following lengthy talks between FIFA and the two national associations on Friday.

items.title: 🤔 Has Brazil really lost its World Cup aura?
items.summary: Brazil used to be synonymous with the World Cup, adored for the way they played as much as for winning more than any other team. Have they lost that magic?
